heading: Remote location filing
label: 1414 Remote location filing

children.label: A
children.text: the Customs Service is satisfied that the participant has the capabilities referred to in paragraph (2)(A) regarding such method of filing; and

children.label: B
children.text: the participant elects to file from the remote location.

children.label: i
children.text: The electronic entry of merchandise.

children.label: ii
children.text: The electronic entry summary of required information.

children.label: iii
children.text: The electronic transmission of invoice information (when required by the Customs Service).

children.label: iv
children.text: The electronic payment of duties, fees, and taxes.

children.label: v
children.text: Such other electronic capabilities within the existing or planned components of the Program as the Secretary shall by regulation require.

children.heading: Restriction on exemption from requirements
children.label: B Restriction on exemption from requirements
children.text: The Customs Service may not permit any exemption or waiver from the requirements established by this section for participation in remote entry filing.

children.heading: Conditions on filing under this section
children.label: 3 Conditions on filing under this section
i
children.text: fails to meet all the compliance requirements and operational standards of remote location filing; or

children.label: ii
children.text: fails to adhere to all applicable laws and regulations.

children.heading: Alternative filing
children.label: 4 Alternative filing
children.text: Any Program participant that is eligible to file entry information electronically from a remote location but chooses not to do so in the case of any entry must file any paper documentation for the entry at the designated location referred to in subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A Program participant that is eligible under subsection (a) to file entry information from a remote location may, if the Customs Service is satisfied that the participant meets the requirements under paragraph (2), also electronically file from the remote location additional information that is required by the Customs Service to be presented before the acceptance of entry summary information and at the time of acceptance of entry summary information.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall publish, and periodically update, a list of those capabilities within the existing and planned components of the Program that a Program participant must have for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: If information electronically acceptable
children.label: A If information electronically acceptable
children.text: A Program participant that is eligible under paragraph (1) to file additional information from a remote location shall electronically file all such information that the Customs Service can accept electronically.

children.heading: Alternative filing
children.label: B Alternative filing
children.text: If the Customs Service cannot accept additional information electronically, the Program participant shall file the paper documentation with respect to the information at the appropriate filing location.

children.label: i
children.text: before , a designated location; andJanuary 1, 1999

children.label: I
children.text: if the paper documentation is required for release, a designated location; or

children.label: II
children.text: if the paper documentation is not required for release, a remote location designated by the Customs Service or a designated location.

children.heading: Other
children.label: D Other
children.text: A Program participant that is eligible under paragraph (1) to file additional information electronically from a remote location but chooses not to do so must file the paper documentation with respect to the information at a designated location.

children.heading: Post-entry summary information
children.label: c Post-entry summary information
children.text: A Program participant that is eligible to file electronically entry information under subsection (a) and additional information under subsection (b) from a remote location may file at any remote location designated by the Customs Service any information required by the Customs Service after entry summary.
The term “designated location” means a customs office located in the customs district designated by the entry filer for purposes of customs examination of the merchandise.
The term “Program participant” means, with respect to an entry of merchandise, any party entitled to make the entry under .section 1484(a)(2)(B) of this title
